issuesFound.title: Toast auto-dismissal cannot be paused or extended
issuesFound.explanation: Toast notifications automatically dismiss after a short duration (3.5s to 6s). Users with cognitive or reading disabilities may not have enough time to read the content, and there is no mechanism to pause the timer or extend the duration.
issuesFound.fix: Line 685: `if (dur > 0) { timerId = setTimeout(() => dismiss(toast), dur); toast.addEventListener('mouseenter', () => clearTimeout(timerId)); toast.addEventListener('mouseleave', () => { timerId = setTimeout(() => dismiss(toast), dur); }); }`

issuesFound.title: Error toasts should not auto-dismiss
issuesFound.explanation: Error notifications auto-dismiss using the same timer as informational toasts. Critical error messages must remain visible until the user acknowledges them or the problem is resolved to ensure they are not missed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 685: `if (dur > 0 && type !== 'error') { timerId = setTimeout(() => dismiss(toast), dur); }`

issuesFound.title: Progress bar missing required ARIA attributes
issuesFound.explanation: The multi-step wizard uses a div as a progress bar but only sets aria-valuenow. It is missing role="progressbar", aria-valuemin, and aria-valuemax, which prevents screen readers from correctly identifying the element and its range.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2543: `_progressBar.setAttribute('role', 'progressbar'); _progressBar.setAttribute('aria-valuemin', '0'); _progressBar.setAttribute('aria-valuemax', '100'); _progressBar.setAttribute('aria-valuenow', String(percent));`

issuesFound.title: Status message aria-live set after content update
issuesFound.explanation: In the discount code module, the aria-live attribute is applied to the status element at the same time as the text content is updated. This often causes screen readers to miss the announcement as the live region was not present in the DOM prior to the change.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2378: `_statusEl = qs('[data-discount-input]'); if (_statusEl) _statusEl.setAttribute('aria-live', 'polite');`

issuesFound.title: Click delegation on non-native elements missing keyboard support
issuesFound.explanation: The theme switcher uses click delegation on elements with the [data-theme-toggle] attribute. If these elements are not native buttons or links, they will not receive keyboard focus or respond to the Enter/Space keys, blocking access for keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Hover-only visual highlight in comparison table

issuesFound.title: Missing keyboard event handler for click on non-interactive element
issuesFound.explanation: The function `_onPointerDown` in the testimonials carousel module attaches a pointerdown event listener to the carousel root element, but does not provide a corresponding keydown handler for Enter or Space keys. This violates WCAG 2.1.1 Keyboard accessibility because users who rely on keyboard navigation cannot activate the carousel controls. Screen reader users and motor-impaired users are affected.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3000: Add a keydown event listener that maps Enter and Space to the same functionality as pointerdown. For example, add: `_rootEl.addEventListener('keydown', _onKeydown);` where `_onKeydown` handles Enter and Space to trigger the same action as pointerdown.

issuesFound.title: Missing ARIA live region for dynamic content injected via innerHTML
issuesFound.explanation: The `suggestionListRenderer.render` function uses `innerHTML` to update the suggestion listbox content without an adjacent ARIA live region to announce the changes to screen readers. This violates WCAG 4.1.3 Status Messages because dynamic search results are not announced. Screen reader users are affected as they may not know that search suggestions have updated.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2500: Ensure the search status region (with id 'search-status') is present and has aria-live="polite". The status region should be updated with the count of suggestions. In the `render` function, after updating innerHTML, call `liveRegion.announce` with the count.

issuesFound.title: Auto-changing content without user control
issuesFound.explanation: The `testimonialsCarousel` module uses `setTimeout` in the `_scheduleNext` function to auto-advance slides without a user-controllable pause mechanism that is always visible and accessible. This violates WCAG 2.2.1 Timing Adjustable because users cannot pause, stop, or hide the auto-advancing carousel. Users with cognitive disabilities or those who need more time to read are affected.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3500: Ensure the play/pause button is always visible and keyboard accessible. The auto-play should respect `prefersReducedMotion` and should be paused by default if reduced motion is preferred. Add a clear pause button with aria-label and ensure it toggles the auto-play.

issuesFound.title: Missing programmatic focus on modal open
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav` module opens a drawer (modal) but does not programmatically move focus into the drawer when it opens. This violates WCAG 2.4.3 Focus Order because keyboard focus remains outside the modal, trapping users. Screen reader and keyboard users are affected as they cannot navigate the modal content.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2000: When the drawer opens, call `focus()` on the first focusable element inside the drawer (or the drawer itself if no focusable children). Use `createFocusTrap` to manage focus within the modal and return focus to the trigger on close.

issuesFound.title: Missing programmatic focus return on modal close
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav` module closes the drawer but does not return focus to the trigger button that opened it. This violates WCAG 2.4.3 Focus Order because keyboard focus is lost when the modal closes. Screen reader and keyboard users are affected.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2100: When closing the drawer, save the trigger element and call `focus()` on it after the close animation completes. Use the `createFocusTrap` deactivation callback to restore focus.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown panels lack `aria-labelledby` linking to their trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown is registered (`register(trigger, panel)`), the script ensures the trigger has `aria-haspopup` and `aria-controls`, but it never assigns `aria-labelledby` on the panel to reference the trigger’s ID. This omission means assistive technologies cannot announce the relationship between the button and the panel content, breaking the required accessible name for the region (WCAG 2.1 ARIA 1.1).

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button missing required ARIA attributes

Without these attributes screen readers cannot convey the button’s purpose or current state, violating WCAG 2.1 (ARIA 1.1) and the “All interactive elements must have an accessible name and state” rule.

issuesFound.title: Search input missing `aria-expanded` attribute for combobox pattern

Assistive technologies therefore cannot announce the open/closed state of the suggestion list, breaching WCAG 2.1 (ARIA 1.1) and the “All interactive elements must expose their state” rule.

fpTitles: Missing keyboard support for non-native interactive elements

issuesFound.title: Missing keyboard support for non-native interactive element
issuesFound.explanation: The `on()` function (lines 10051–10800) attaches click event listeners to non-native interactive elements (e.g., `<div>`, `<span>`) without providing matching keyboard support (Enter/Space). This violates WCAG 2.1.1 (Keyboard) because these elements cannot be activated via keyboard, excluding keyboard-only and screen reader users.

fpTitles: Missing keyboard support for non-native interactive element

issuesFound.title: Missing keyboard support for non-native interactive elements
issuesFound.explanation: The `createElement` function (lines 1050-1144) attaches click event listeners to elements via the `attrs` object when an attribute key starts with "on" and the value is a function. However, it does not attach corresponding keyboard event handlers (keydown/keyup) for Enter and Space keys. This means that non-native interactive elements (e.g., `<div>` or `<span>` with click handlers) will not be operable by keyboard users, violating WCAG 2.1.1 (Keyboard).

issuesFound.title: Missing keyboard support for non-native interactive elements in `delegate` function
issuesFound.explanation: The `delegate` function (lines 680-709) attaches a click event listener to a parent element and invokes the handler when a child element matching `childSelector` is clicked. However, it does not attach a corresponding keyboard event listener to handle Enter and Space key presses. This means that non-native interactive elements (e.g., `<div>` or `<span>` with click handlers) will not be operable by keyboard users, violating WCAG 2.1.1 (Keyboard).

issuesFound.title: Missing keyboard support for non-native interactive elements in `on` function
issuesFound.explanation: The `on` function (lines 640-677) attaches event listeners to elements, including click events. However, it does not automatically attach corresponding keyboard event handlers (keydown/keyup) for Enter and Space keys when a click handler is added to a non-native interactive element (e.g., `<div>` or `<span>`). This violates WCAG 2.1.1 (Keyboard) as these elements will not be operable by keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Missing keyboard support for non-native interactive elements in `onOnce` function
issuesFound.explanation: The `onOnce` function (lines 670-679) attaches a one-time click event listener to an element. However, it does not attach a corresponding keyboard event handler for Enter and Space keys. This means that non-native interactive elements (e.g., `<div>` or `<span>` with click handlers) will not be operable by keyboard users, violating WCAG 2.1.1 (Keyboard).

issuesFound.title: Missing keyboard support for non-native interactive elements in `mobileNav._onTriggerClick`
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav._onTriggerClick` function (line 5267) is triggered by a click event on the mobile navigation trigger button. However, if the trigger is not a native `<button>` element (e.g., a `<div>` or `<span>` styled as a button), it will not be keyboard operable. This violates WCAG 2.1.1 (Keyboard) as keyboard users will not be able to open the mobile navigation menu.

issuesFound.title: Missing keyboard support for non-native interactive elements in `dropdownMenu._onTriggerClick`
issuesFound.explanation: The `dropdownMenu._onTriggerClick` function (line 5600) is triggered by a click event on dropdown trigger buttons. However, if the trigger is not a native `<button>` element (e.g., a `<div>` or `<span>` styled as a button), it will not be keyboard operable. This violates WCAG 2.1.1 (Keyboard) as keyboard users will not be able to open dropdown menus.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion non-button triggers lack Enter/Space activation
issuesFound.explanation: When FAQ triggers are not native `<button>` or `<summary>` elements, the script assigns them `role="button"` and `tabindex="0"` but does not handle Enter or Space key events to activate them. Keyboard-only users cannot expand/collapse answers using standard keys. This fails WCAG 2.1.1 (Keyboard).
issuesFound.fix: Line 1 (_onKeydown function): Insert the following case before `default: break;`: `case KEYS.ENTER: case KEYS.SPACE: { e.preventDefault(); const item = faqRegistry.getAll().find(i => i.trigger === trigger); if (item) toggle(item); break; }`.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The search input uses `role="combobox"` but does not maintain `aria-expanded` to indicate whether the suggestions popup is visible. Assistive technologies cannot convey the visibility state of the listbox to users. This fails WCAG 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value).
issuesFound.fix: Line 1 (_open function): Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `_searchState.open = true;`. Line 1 (_close function): Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` immediately after `_searchState.open = false;`. Line 1 (init function): After `suggestionListRenderer.mount(_inputEl);`, add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: pricing switch role missing aria-checked
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle is assigned `role="switch"` when not a native button, but `aria-checked` is never set to reflect the current state (monthly vs annual). Screen readers cannot announce whether the switch is on or off. This fails WCAG 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value).
issuesFound.fix: Line 1 (_syncToggleState function): Inside the `_periodBtns.forEach` loop, add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));` (or `aria-selected` if using a tab pattern). Line 1 (init function): After `if (_toggleEl.tagName !== 'BUTTON') { ... setAttr(_toggleEl, 'role', 'switch'); }`, add `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-checked', String(_pricingState.period === 'year'));`. Also update this in `_syncToggleState` for the switch element.

issuesFound.explanation: The upload zone acts as a button that reveals a file list when files are selected. The zone lacks `aria-expanded` to indicate the visibility state of the file list region, and the file list lacks a defined relationship (via `aria-controls`) to the zone. This degrades the experience for screen reader users (WCAG 4.1.2 best practice).
issuesFound.fix: Line 1 (bind function): After creating `listEl`, add `const listId = srUtils.ensureId(listEl, 'upload-list'); setAttr(zoneEl, 'aria-controls', listId);`. Line 1 (_processFiles function): After files are added and the list is shown, add `setAttr(zoneEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` (and set to `'false'` in `clear` function).

issuesFound.title: Search combobox input missing aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The search input is assigned role="combobox" and aria-haspopup="listbox", but the code never sets aria-expanded to indicate whether the suggestion popup is open or closed. Screen reader users cannot tell when the listbox is visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1765: After `setAttr(input, 'aria-haspopup', 'listbox');`, add: `setAttr(input, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` Then in the `_open` function (around line 1785), add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` and in `_close` (around line 1792), add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Search suggestion items missing aria-selected update
issuesFound.explanation: In `setActiveIndex`, the code toggles the CSS active class on suggestion items but does not update the `aria-selected` attribute to reflect which option is selected. Screen readers cannot identify the currently highlighted suggestion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1813: Inside the `forEach` loop in `setActiveIndex`, add: `setAttr(item, 'aria-selected', String(isActive));`

issuesFound.title: Contact form first name input missing autocomplete attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The input with selector `#contact-first-name` (bound in `contactForm.init`) collects personal data (given name) but lacks an autocomplete attribute. This prevents browsers from assisting users with autofill and fails WCAG 1.3.5.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2874: After `const field = qs(cfg.selector, _formEl);`, add: `if (field && cfg.selector.includes('first-name')) field.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'given-name');`

issuesFound.title: Contact form last name input missing autocomplete attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The input with selector `#contact-last-name` collects personal data (family name) but lacks an autocomplete attribute.

issuesFound.title: Contact form email input missing autocomplete attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The input with selector `#contact-email` collects email address but lacks an autocomplete attribute.

issuesFound.title: Newsletter email input missing autocomplete attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletter form email input (`#newsletter-email`) collects email addresses but does not have autocomplete="email" set, preventing browser autofill assistance.

issuesFound.title: Assertive live region has conflicting role
issuesFound.explanation: The assertive live region wrapper is created with `role="status"` (which implies polite priority) but also `aria-live="assertive"`. These attributes conflict because `role="status"` is inherently polite, while `aria-live="assertive"` demands immediate interruption.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1200: In the `makeRegion` function for the assertive region, change `role: 'status'` to `role: 'alert'`.

issuesFound.title: Product filter tabs missing aria-selected
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab buttons use a roving tabindex pattern, but the code never sets `aria-selected="true"` on the active tab or `aria-selected="false"` on inactive tabs. Screen readers cannot identify which filter category is currently applied.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2278: In `_syncTabAriaStates`, inside the `forEach` loop, add: `setAttr(btn, 'aria-selected', String(isActive));`

issuesFound.title: Wizard progressbar missing aria-valuemin and aria-valuemax
issuesFound.explanation: The `_updateProgress` function sets `aria-valuenow` and `aria-valuetext` on the progressbar element, but never ensures `aria-valuemin` and `aria-valuemax` are set. Screen readers cannot calculate the percentage completion without these bounds.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3023: After checking `if (!_progressBar) return;`, add: `if (!_progressBar.hasAttribute('aria-valuemin')) _progressBar.setAttribute('aria-valuemin', '0'); if (!_progressBar.hasAttribute('aria-valuemax')) _progressBar.setAttribute('aria-valuemax', '100');`

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer missing aria-modal
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer (`_drawer`) acts as a modal dialog (traps focus, blocks interaction with background) when open, but lacks `aria-modal="true"`. Screen readers may not announce it as a modal and users might not realize the background is inert.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1390: After `_initialised = true;` and checking that `_drawer` exists, add: `_drawer.setAttribute('aria-modal', 'true');` Also ensure it has `role="dialog"` or is a `<dialog>` element.

issuesFound.title: Search clear button potentially missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The search clear button (`_clearBtn`) is selected from the DOM, but the code never verifies or sets an accessible name (aria-label or text content). If the HTML lacks text content or aria-label, screen users won't know its purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1735: After `_clearBtn = qs(cfg.clearSelector);`, add: `if (_clearBtn && !_clearBtn.getAttribute('aria-label') && !_clearBtn.textContent.trim()) _clearBtn.setAttribute('aria-label', STRINGS.labels.clearSearch || 'Clear search');`

issuesFound.title: Broken .sr-only class makes content visible instead of hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class is supposed to visually hide content while keeping it accessible to screen readers, but the CSS does the opposite—it sets position: static, width: auto, overflow: visible, and clip: auto, which makes content fully visible. The comment states "visually hidden but announced by AT" but the implementation contradicts this. Content meant to be screen-reader-only (like skip link targets, form labels) will be visible to all users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 300-310: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; margin: -1px; padding: 0; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Root font-size set to 12px below recommended minimum
issuesFound.explanation: The html element has font-size: 12px, which sets the base font size below the recommended 16px (1rem). Since body uses font-size: var(--font-size-base) which equals 1rem, all body text renders at 12px. This significantly reduces readability for users with low vision or reading difficulties, violating WCAG 1.4.12 Text Spacing best practices.
issuesFound.fix: Line 101: font-size: 16px;

issuesFound.title: Paragraph line-height set to 1 (below minimum 1.5)
issuesFound.explanation: The p selector has line-height: 1, which is significantly below the recommended minimum of 1.5 for body text. This cramped line spacing makes text harder to read, especially for users with dyslexia, low vision, or cognitive disabilities. WCAG 1.4.12 Text Spacing requires that text remain readable when users adjust spacing.
issuesFound.fix: Line 153: line-height: var(--line-height-normal);

issuesFound.title: Empty prefers-reduced-motion media query fails to suppress transitions
issuesFound.explanation: The @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block is completely empty despite the comment stating "all transitions and animations suppressed for users who prefer reduced motion." Multiple elements (.skip-link, .btn, .hero-image img, .feature-card, .product-card, .stat-item, .pricing-card) have transitions defined but no corresponding rules in this media query to disable them. Users with vestibular disorders may experience motion-induced discomfort.

issuesFound.title: Suggestion type badge hidden from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The suggestion type badge (showing "Product", "Section", "Page", "FAQ", "Recent", etc.) has aria-hidden="true", hiding this categorization information from screen readers. Users who cannot see the visual badge will not know what type of result each suggestion is, which could affect their ability to choose the correct result efficiently.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer missing focus trap and focus management
issuesFound.explanation: The _trap variable is declared but never initialized with createFocusTrap(). When the mobile navigation drawer opens, focus is not moved into it and no focus trap is activated, allowing keyboard users to Tab outside the drawer to content beneath it. Focus is also not returned to the trigger button when the drawer closes. This violates WCAG 2.4.3 Focus Order for modal dialogs.

issuesFound.title: Toast notifications use incorrect ARIA role for non-error messages
issuesFound.explanation: Non-error toast notifications are created with role="alert" combined with aria-live="polite". The role="alert" is inherently assertive and will always interrupt screen readers, contradicting the polite live setting. This creates inconsistent announcements for success/info/warning toasts.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer missing focus management on open
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer opens, focus is not programmatically moved into it. Keyboard users remain focused on the trigger button while the drawer is open, making it difficult to navigate the newly revealed content. This violates WCAG 2.4.3 (Focus Order).

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer missing focus trap activation
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav module declares a _trap variable for focus trapping but never creates or activates the focus trap when the drawer opens. Keyboard users can Tab outside the drawer while it is open, violating WCAG 2.1.2 (No Keyboard Trap) and WCAG 2.4.3 (Focus Order).

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu not returning focus to trigger on close
issuesFound.explanation: The _closeInstance() function accepts a returnFocus parameter but never uses it to return focus to the trigger button. When users close a dropdown via Escape, focus is not restored, violating WCAG 2.4.3 (Focus Order).
